title: Mass-Energy Equivalence: E = mc²
definition: Mass-energy equivalence states that mass and energy are the same physical entity, convertible into each other. The equation E = mc² shows that energy (E) equals mass (m) times the speed of light (c) squared.

difficulty_levels.beginner: Mass can turn into energy, like in the sun or nuclear power plants
difficulty_levels.intermediate: Mass is concentrated energy; c² is a huge conversion factor
difficulty_levels.expert: Invariant mass includes both rest mass and kinetic energy contributions
